background: Each year, cardiologists surgically repair thousands of clogged coronary arteries only to have many of these arteries narrow again (restenose) soon after surgery. A study sponsored by the NIH was conducted to help determine whether infection with a common type of virus, cytomegalovirus (CMY), contributed to coronary restenosis. Forty-nine of the subjects showed serological evidence of CMV infection, while 26 showed no such evidence. In the CMV+ group, 21 individuals restenosed within 6 months of atherectomy. In the CMV — group, 2 individuals restenosed.

task.query: Calculate the risk in the CMV+ group and the CMV- group, and find the risk difference.
task.answer: Risk in the CMV+group \( \hat{p}_1=\frac{21}{49}=0.4286 \) Risk in the CVM - group \( \hat{p}_2=\frac{2}{26}=0.0769 \) Risk difference \( \hat{p}_1-\hat{p}_2=0.4286-0.0769=0.3517 \)
task.method: 事件发生概率及独立性

task.query: Calculate the 95% confidence interval for the risk difference using the plus-four method.
Explain the confidence interval in lay terms.
task.answer: We have 95% confidence in the technique used to calculate the confidence interval. The technique says that CMV increases the risk of restenosis by between 14.7% and 50.2%.

task.query: Compare the results calculated by WinPepi Compare2.exe with the plus-four method.
task.answer: DIFFERENCE [A minus B]=0.352 SE=0.088 Large-sample method (Fleiss), continuity-corrected: 90% C.I.=0.178 to 0.526 95% C.I.=0.150 to 0.553 99% C.I.=0.096 to 0.608 Wilson's score method: Not continuity-corrected (Newcombe's method 10): 90% C.I.=0.181 to 0.479 95% C.I.=\mathbf{0.143} to \mathbf{0.501} 99% C.I.=0.067 to 0.542 Continuity-corrected (Newcombe's method 11): 90% C.I.=0.156 to 0.492 95% C.I.=0.118 to 0.513 99% C.I.=0.042 to 0.552 The 95% confidence interval by the Wilson score method, not continuity-corrected (bold face) most closely corresponds with our plus-four method.
